name: openai
summary: Official Python SDK for the OpenAI API — call GPT-4, DALL-E, Whisper, and Embeddings with a clean async-first interface.

story.problem: You want to add AI capabilities — text generation, image creation, speech recognition, or embeddings — to your Python application without managing HTTP requests and response parsing yourself.
story.mental_model: The SDK wraps REST endpoints in typed Python objects. Create an OpenAI client, then call client.chat.completions.create() with a list of message dicts. The response is a structured object with .choices[0].message.content. Everything has an async equivalent.
story.when_to_use: Integrating GPT-4/3.5 into apps, generating embeddings for semantic search, transcribing audio with Whisper, generating images with DALL-E, or building agentic workflows with tool calling.
story.when_not_to_use: When you need to run models locally (use ollama or transformers), when cost is a hard constraint for high-volume tasks, or when you need a specific open-source model.

alternatives.name: anthropic
alternatives.reason: Claude models — often better for long documents and coding

alternatives.name: langchain
alternatives.reason: Orchestration framework for complex LLM pipelines

nodes.label: chat.completions.create()
nodes.summary: Send messages, get back a completion

nodes.label: Streaming
nodes.summary: Stream tokens as they're generated with stream=True

tasks.title: Send a chat message and get a response

steps.label: Basic chat completion
Stream response tokens
steps.code:
with client.chat.completions.stream(
    model='gpt-4o',
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Write a haiku about Python.'}]
) as stream:
    for text in stream.text_stream:
        print(text, end='', flush=True)
print()  # newline at end

steps.label: Create embeddings
steps.code:
import numpy as np

texts = ['Python is great', 'I love async programming', 'Coffee is essential']
response = client.embeddings.create(model='text-embedding-3-small', input=texts)
vectors = [r.embedding for r in response.data]  # list of 1536-dim float lists
